text.title: Legal Compliance Routing and Overview Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-legal-compliance` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Produce a written routing decision-record naming which sub-methodology applies (`ops-legal-basics` for foundation, `ops-legal-compliance-checklist` for stage-gated implementation). Skipping the routing record is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a routing record, work duplicates between the foundation and the implementation tracker. The routing record names the sub-methodology and the owner of each sub-track.

rule.statement: List every regulation applicable to the business (GDPR, CCPA, CAN-SPAM, COPPA, ADA, PCI-DSS) with an explicit applies/not-applies status per regulation. 'Will-check-later' status is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unknown applicability is the dominant cause of inadvertent non-compliance. Forcing applies/not-applies status surfaces gaps before regulators do.

rule.statement: Inventory ToS, Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy, and Refund Policy with `last_reviewed` per policy. Any policy with `last_reviewed` > 12 months is treated as stale and routed to refresh.
rule.rationale: Policies drift faster than products. Stale policies expose the operator to enforcement based on outdated text that the team no longer follows.

rule.statement: Maintain a separate contractor / vendor contract inventory with NDA, scope, payment terms, and IP clause per row. Treating contractor agreements as out-of-scope of compliance is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Contractor and vendor breaches cascade into the operator's compliance posture (SOC 2, GDPR processor obligations). The vendor track is part of compliance, not a side channel.

rule.statement: Review the routing record quarterly and update which sub-methodology applies as the business grows (entity change, new market, new payment processor). Routing once and never again is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Compliance applicability changes with business growth (new market triggers new privacy law; new payment method triggers PCI-DSS). Quarterly review keeps routing accurate.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
